name: Tenadam (Feverfew / Rue of Grace)
preparation: Infuse 3-4 leaves in hot water with local organic honey, or crush fresh leaves and breathe in the aroma for immediate sinus relief.
scientificBasis: Contains parthenolide which inhibits serotonin release and reduces inflammatory spasms in blood vessels.
warning: Do not consume in high quantities during pregnancy.

name: Damakese (Indigenous Ocimum lamiifolium)
preparation: Squeeze fresh leaves to extract the juice and drink raw, or inhale vapor from leaves boiled in pure spring water.
scientificBasis: Rich in essential oils (sabinene, pinene) with proven anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties.
warning: Bitter taste; consume with water or tea.

name: Moringa (Shiferaw)
preparation: Mix 1 teaspoon of dried organic moringa leaf powder into warm water or teff-based porridge during morning/evening meals.
scientificBasis: High content of plant protein, vitamin C, iron, and calcium to counteract maternal and athletic undernutrition during strict fasting phases.

name: Tsedey Blend (Ginger, Mint, & Cinnamon)
preparation: Steep grated ginger, fresh field mint, and a stick of cinnamon in boiling water for 7 minutes. Drink warm before sleep.
scientificBasis: Gingerols and menthol soothe the gastrointestinal tract and lower systemic stress hormone levels.

name: Clarified Kibe Somatic Grounding
preparation: Warm spiced clarified butter (kibe) massaged into the cervical spine (neck) and lumbar region, followed by a hot compress.
scientificBasis: Spiced butter contains anti-inflammatory compounds that penetrate cutaneous tissue and increase muscle elasticity.
warning: Ensure butter is warm, not hot.
